claim_status: Benchmark public-claim bundle template only; no browser benchmark run, no benchmark result, no competitor result, no trace captured, no raw sample, no memory result, no energy result, no Chrome-class claim, no faster claim, no lower-memory claim, no lower-energy claim, no public performance claim, no compatibility claim, no security claim, no accessibility claim, no production claim, and no daily-driver claim.
claim_level: Level 3 public claim candidate template only
claim_text: No public claim is approved by this template.

evidence_inputs.missing_proof: No owner-approved Tier L, Tier M, and Tier H hardware set exists for a public claim.

evidence_inputs.input: approved clean OS and update controls
evidence_inputs.missing_proof: No clean image, update freeze, driver freeze, thermal stability, network isolation, or owner-approved OS-control proof exists.
No reviewed representative offline corpus, live/replay corpus policy, or workload denominator approval exists.
No implemented browser launch runner has launched Turing or a competitor browser and produced raw samples.
No benchmark-ready browser pins with owner-reviewed versions, command lines, profiles, settings, update state, sandbox, site isolation, and lifecycle settings exist.
No ETW, WPR, Perfetto, runner log, browser stdout/stderr, memory, energy, screenshot, video, raw-sample, or artifact-index package exists.
No mixed-state or all-live 30-tab browser run, process topology, tab lifecycle log, discard/revival record, or state-loss record exists.

No runner-generated raw sample set, sample count, warmup record, confidence interval from measured browser data, uncertainty statement, outlier policy execution, owner-reviewed statistics analysis, or statistical summary from real samples exists.
No owner-reviewed equivalence statement covers workload, cache, lifecycle, security, sandbox, site isolation, extension, profile, process model, and power settings.
No owner-approved exact claim text, expiry, rerun trigger, unsupported behavior, failure denominator, or public copy diff exists.

statistical_controls: record raw sample count for every target and scenario
record warmup policy, excluded warmup samples, and measured sample order
record repetitions, randomization, cold/warm/repeat-view policy, and cache state
record confidence intervals or explicit uncertainty statements before summary language
record outlier policy before the run and retain rejected samples in the artifact bundle
record failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, and revived cases in the denominator
record effect size, variance, and practical significance instead of ranking by one synthetic score
record hardware, OS, driver, firmware, power, thermal, display, and network control IDs beside every statistic
record the statistics-analysis plan ID used to interpret raw samples and block claim bundles when that plan is missing, stale, or broader than the evidence
equivalence_controls: compare only equivalent workload versions, fixture hashes, routes, viewports, DPR, refresh rate, and input scripts
compare only equivalent browser channel, profile, cache, extension, account, sync, and update-state controls
compare only equivalent sandbox, site-isolation, certificate, JIT, memory-saver, energy-saver, background-throttling, and security settings
label any unsupported or disabled feature in the denominator instead of excluding it
label local lab metrics separately from CrUX, field telemetry, or public web measurements
preserve process topology, tab lifecycle, profile, origin, site, frame, and document-epoch identity for interpretation
keep Turing, Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari, Servo, and Ladybird settings documented separately when included
block public comparison when competitor versions, benchmark suites, OS images, drivers, or hardware differ outside the reviewed claim scope
denominator_controls: retain every failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, revived, and state-loss case
retain unsupported APIs, disabled suites, missing subtests, and incompatible pages in the denominator
retain browser launch failures and wrong-target launches as denominator failures
retain trace, artifact-finalization, cleanup, and profile-isolation failures as denominator failures
retain tab discard, memory saver, energy saver, background throttling, and lifecycle policy effects
retain security mitigation differences as blockers or explicit invalidators for public claims
retain accessibility, DevTools, extension, profile, and agent overhead when the claim touches those workflows
publish denominator size, exclusions, unsupported behavior, and residual risk with any approved claim
overhead_controls: include accessibility tree, screen-reader, high-contrast, reduced-motion, and keyboard overhead when claiming everyday or accessible performance
include crash recovery and state restoration overhead when claiming stability or recovery performance
include DevTools, tracing, diagnostics, source maps, protocol, and debugger overhead when claiming developer performance
include extension, Plug-in, automation, and agent overhead when claiming those workflows
include profile, session, storage, credential, and privacy-boundary overhead when claiming daily-driver behavior
include updater, package, and incident-response overhead only after those systems exist and are in scope
expiry_policy: expire competitor comparison claims after 30 days by default
expire nightly, dev, canary, or technology-preview comparison claims after 14 days by default
expire immediately when a competitor stable channel updates
expire immediately when benchmark suite versions, corpus hashes, route maps, or input scripts change
expire immediately when hardware, OS image, driver, firmware, power, thermal, display, network, sandbox, lifecycle, or browser-version inputs change
expire immediately when an omitted failure, unsupported case, security difference, or denominator error is discovered
remove expired claims from public docs, release notes, website copy, README files, issue templates, benchmark dashboards, and market material until rerun evidence exists
publication_controls: publish only exact owner-approved claim text
publish supported hardware, operating systems, browser versions, workload versions, sample counts, and expiration date
publish raw artifact bundle hash, trace package hash, redaction status, retention decision, and reviewer identity
publish unsupported behavior, residual risk, denominator size, failed cases, and excluded cases
publish rerun triggers and removal policy beside the claim

block fastest, lower-memory, lower-energy, Chrome-class, daily-driver, production, beta, stable, security, compatibility, or accessibility language unless the approved claim explicitly and narrowly supports it
rejection_rules: reject any claim bundle without runner-generated browser-run raw samples
reject any claim bundle without trace and artifact package hashes
reject any claim bundle without benchmark-ready browser pins and competitor version evidence
reject any claim bundle without denominator records for failures, unsupported cases, crashes, timeouts, cancellations, discards, revivals, and state loss
reject any claim bundle that hides security, sandbox, site-isolation, cache, lifecycle, profile, extension, or account differences
reject any claim bundle that uses different benchmark suite versions, corpus hashes, viewport classes, refresh rates, OS images, drivers, or hardware outside the approved scope
reject any claim bundle with missing sample count, uncertainty, outlier policy, warmup policy, or raw artifact hash
reject any claim bundle without a registry reference to the statistics-analysis plan used to interpret raw samples
reject any claim bundle with public text broader than the approved evidence
reject any claim bundle without owner and independent reviewer approval after the latest artifact change
unsupported_behavior: no production, beta, stable, release, or daily-driver claim is supported
missing_proof: approved Tier L, Tier M, and Tier H hardware manifests or narrower owner-approved claim scope
approved clean OS image and update-control manifests
reviewed representative offline corpus and any selected live/replay corpus policy
implemented browser launch runner and runner-managed local server artifacts
owner-reviewed benchmark-ready browser pins and complete competitor local/version evidence
runner-generated raw samples, trace package, memory snapshots, power/energy samples, failure records, and artifact hashes
owner-reviewed statistics analysis generated from the referenced statistics-analysis plan, sample count, uncertainty, outlier policy, warmup policy, and failure denominator
owner-approved exact public claim text, expiry, rerun triggers, and public copy diff
